name: python3-venv
title: python3-venv
summary: venv module for python3 (default python3 version)
description:
venv module for python3 (default python3 version)
This package contains the venv module for the Python language (default python3
version).
.
The venv module provides support for creating lightweight "virtual
environments" with their own site directories, optionally isolated from system
site directories. Each virtual environment has its own Python binary (which
matches the version of the binary that was used to create this environment)
and can have its own independent set of installed Python packages in its site
directories.
.
This package is a dependency package, which depends on Debian's default
Python 3 version's venv module (currently v3.13).
use_cases: When you need Kali Linux, use python3-venv because venv module for python3 (default python3 version).
When the engagement is in the install phase, use python3-venv as a cataloged Kali Linux tool with operator ACCEPT/CANCEL gating.
When chaining Kali Linux tools, pair python3-venv with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `python3-venv --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.
